title: Three Lectures On Topological Phases Of Matter
canonical_scope: Lecture One proof-grade exemplar, with promoted Lecture Two and Lecture Three flagship theorem families, explicit cited-paper recovery routes, and a topic-level future buffer.

section_map.title: Relativistic Dispersion In One Space Dimension
section_map.summary: Linearization near a Fermi point yields chiral low-energy modes in 1D.

section_map.title: Three Dimensions
section_map.summary: Generic isolated two-band crossings in 3D are locally Weyl points.

section_map.title: The Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem
section_map.summary: The total topological charge of isolated Weyl nodes on a lattice vanishes.

section_map.title: The Berry Connection
section_map.summary: Occupied states define a line bundle whose curvature measures node charge.

section_map.title: Some Examples
section_map.summary: Toy Hamiltonians create, move, split, and annihilate Weyl nodes.

section_map.title: Band Crossing At The Fermi Energy
section_map.summary: A physically relevant semimetal requires the crossing to sit at the Fermi level.

section_map.title: Including Spin
section_map.summary: Spin doubles bands and changes symmetry bookkeeping.

section_map.title: A System With Many Bands
section_map.summary: Berry connections become non-abelian before projection to filled states.

section_map.title: Two Dimensions
section_map.summary: Generic 2D Dirac crossings require special structure or symmetry.

section_map.title: Weyl Fermions And Fermi Arcs
section_map.summary: Surface spectra terminate at the projections of bulk Weyl nodes.

section_map.title: Gapless Boundary Modes From Dirac Fermions
section_map.summary: Mass-domain-wall intuition explains protected boundary-localized gapless modes.

section_map.title: Discrete Lattice Symmetries and Massless Dirac Fermions
section_map.summary: Massless Dirac points survive in lattices only when symmetry data enforce them.

section_map.title: Simple Examples Of Band Hamiltonians
section_map.summary: Periodic lattice Hamiltonians exhibit fermion doubling in explicit models.

section_map.title: Chern-Simons Effective Action
section_map.summary: Lecture Two begins from the effective Chern-Simons description of the integer quantum Hall effect.

section_map.title: Quantization Of The Chern-Simons Coupling
section_map.summary: Gauge invariance on closed spacetime forces the Chern-Simons level to be integral.

section_map.title: Quantization Of The Hall Conductivity
section_map.summary: The Hall response coefficient is tied to the quantized Chern-Simons level.

section_map.title: Relation To Band Topology
section_map.summary: The macroscopic Hall coefficient is related to the TKNN invariant of the filled-state bundle.

section_map.title: Proof Of The Equivalence
section_map.summary: Witten sketches why the many-body twisted-boundary Berry curvature agrees with the band-theory Chern number.

section_map.title: Edge States And Anomaly Inflow
section_map.summary: Boundary chiral modes cancel the gauge variation of the bulk Chern-Simons term.

section_map.title: The Charge Pump
section_map.summary: The Laughlin-Thouless pump interpretation explains boundary spectral flow and charge transport.

section_map.title: Joining Valence And Conduction Bands
section_map.summary: Edge-localized modes must interpolate continuously between valence and conduction bands.

section_map.title: More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect
section_map.summary: A first pass at fractional Hall response introduces emergent gauge fields and non-return after a $2\pi$ pump.

section_map.title: More On Fermi Arcs
section_map.summary: Fermi arcs are revisited via auxiliary quantum Hall systems and projected momentum-space topology.

section_map.title: More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect
section_map.summary: Lecture Three develops fractionally charged quasiparticles, Wilson loops, and topological ground-state degeneracy.

section_map.title: More On Edge States Of The Integer Quantum Hall Effect
section_map.summary: A continuum parity-anomaly picture and the Jackiw-Rebbi domain-wall mode supply a conceptual edge-mode proof.

section_map.title: Haldane's Model Of Graphene
section_map.summary: Graphene Dirac points, symmetry protection, and Haldane masses realize anomalous Hall physics on the lattice.
